title: Maple Heights
description: Exploring the suburb. The strip mall with medieval items. The shopkeeper who knows about the portal.

lines: The suburb existed in a state of aggressive normalcy. Every house was a variation on the same house. Every lawn was the same shade of green.
The sky was blue, but it was the wrong blue — not wrong in a way you could point to, but wrong in a way you could feel.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: It looks like a village that's been through several stages of middle management. Someone standardized this.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: Someone standardized the trees. Same species, same age, same pruning. That's not natural growth.

steps.text: A kid on a bicycle cruised past without stopping. They looked at the party, looked at the sword, looked at the scrubs, looked at the trumpet, and pedaled faster.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: That child did not seem surprised by us.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: That child was not surprised by anything. That was a child who has seen things.

steps.text: The strip mall had a pizza place, a candle shop, a frozen yogurt place, and — inexplicably — an armory. YE OLDE MEDIEVAL SURPLUS, read the sign.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: Everything's half off. We got a huge shipment from... I don't know, the warehouse? It just shows up.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: From wherever. You're from the other side of the portal, aren't you. Don't worry about it. People come through sometimes.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: This HOA. What are they, exactly?

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: Honey, you are five people in costumes carrying medieval weaponry in a suburb where the mailboxes are already alive.

lines.speaker: Shopkeeper
lines.text: You are maximum variance. The Coordinator is going to find you within the day. Stock up first.
